summary: Iteration 17: Full assessment flow E2E testing. Found and fixed 8 critical bugs - most were in the public assessment client flow (assessmentApi.js wrong paths, missing type handlers, wrong question field names). All flows now work end-to-end: template edit → toggle publish → assign → client fills → auto-save → submit → admin sees result → PDF export.

critical.issue: TemplateUpdate model missing 'published' field → AttributeError when sending description-only updates (sections=None triggered elif payload.published is not None which crashed with AttributeError)
TemplateIn.description and TemplateUpdate.description only accepted dict but TemplateEditorV2 sends string → 422 Validation Error on every template save via the frontend editor
critical.status: FIXED: changed to str | dict | None

ui_bugs.issue: ALL 3 public assessment API paths were wrong (used session-based /sessions/{token} paths instead of public /{token} paths). loadSession used GET /assessment/sessions/{token} → 405 Method Not Allowed. saveAnswers used PATCH (should be POST). pdfUrl used /export.pdf (should be /export)
ui_bugs.status: FIXED: corrected all paths to use public token-based endpoints

ui_bugs.issue: const domains = template.domains || [] — non-KN3 templates use 'sections' field not 'domains'. Domain cards never showed for non-KN3 templates.
Uses q.prompt for question text label — KN3 uses 'prompt' but non-KN3 templates use 'text'. Question text empty for non-KN3 templates.

Only handled old types: single_choice, multi_choice, yes_no, scale_1_5. Missing handlers for normalized types: select, multiselect, yesno, textarea, scale. 'select' and 'textarea' fell to generic text input → no options rendered for select questions.
ui_bugs.status: FIXED: added handlers for all normalized types

ui_bugs.issue: Summary shows q.prompt for question label — same issue as AssessmentQuestion. Non-KN3 question labels empty in summary.
renderVal only handled: single_choice, multi_choice, yes_no, scale_1_5. Missing select, multiselect, yesno, scale → summary showed raw values (e.g. 'manufacturing') instead of labels ('Manufaktur').
ui_bugs.status: FIXED: added handlers for all normalized types

integration_issues.issue: assessmentApi.deleteAttachment calls DELETE /assessment/{token}/attachments/{attId} but this endpoint doesn't exist in backend (only auth-based DELETE /sessions/{session_id}/attachments/{attId} exists). Attachment deletion in public flow will fail.
integration_issues.status: NOT FIXED - needs backend to add public token-based attachment delete OR restrict to auth-only in UI

integration_issues.issue: TemplateEditorV2 sends description as string, while the correct format is dict {id:..., en:...}. Backend fixed to accept both, but frontend should also be fixed for data consistency.
integration_issues.status: PARTIALLY FIXED - backend accepts string now, but frontend ideally should send dict

action_items: Fix TemplateEditorV2.jsx handleSave to send description as dict {id: cat, en: cat} instead of string (for consistency with TemplateIn model design)
Add public token-based DELETE /api/assessment/{token}/attachments/{attId} endpoint in backend, OR remove delete attachment functionality from AssessmentClient component
Review all other question-type consumers in codebase to ensure they also handle both old (single_choice, yes_no, multi_choice) and new normalized (select, yesno, multiselect) type names
critical_code_review_comments: AssessmentClient.jsx: The 'domains' variable should use template.sections || template.domains to handle both template formats consistently. Applied fix in this iteration.

assessmentApi.js: All public assessment API paths were pointing to session-based (auth) endpoints. This completely broke the public assessment taking flow. Root cause: the API was refactored at some point but assessmentApi.js was not updated.

AssessmentQuestion.jsx + AssessmentClient.jsx Summary: Both use question.prompt for text display but only KN3 template uses 'prompt'. All other templates use 'text'. Both places needed q.text || q.prompt.
Type handling is duplicated between AssessmentQuestion.jsx and Summary's renderVal — consider extracting to a shared utility function.
TemplateUpdate pydantic model was missing 'published' field despite the update handler trying to access payload.published. This should have been caught in code review.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all critical flows working; minor deleteAttachment endpoint missing for public flow)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test sessions (TEST_Client_Iter17 x4) during testing. Cleaned up. Submitted 1 'Client Demo / Kebutuhan Sistem Informasi' session as part of E2E test (status=submitted, token=98918948-da10-4ab4-a418-1df35b915a84).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All major assessment flow bugs have been fixed in this iteration. The public assessment flow (token-based via /assessment/:token) is now fully working. Key files changed: assessmentApi.js (all paths), AssessmentClient.jsx (domains field, Summary renderVal, q.text||q.prompt), AssessmentQuestion.jsx (type handlers, q.text||q.prompt), assessment.py backend (TemplateUpdate model). Remaining issues: (1) deleteAttachment public endpoint missing, (2) TemplateEditorV2 sends description as string not dict. The KN3 template (82 questions, 14 domains) exists and is published. Template editing (edit → auto-unpublish → toggle publish) works correctly. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_assessment_flow_iter17.py covers full flow.

assessmentApi_wrong_paths.root_cause: The assessmentApi.js was written/updated to use auth-based session endpoints (/assessment/sessions/{token}) but the public assessment flow should use token-based endpoints (/assessment/{token}). Likely a refactoring where the API route structure changed but assessmentApi.js was not updated.
assessmentApi_wrong_paths.reproduction: Navigate to /assessment/{any_valid_token} - page shows 'Link Tidak Valid' error
assessmentApi_wrong_paths.mitigation: Fixed all 4 functions in assessmentApi.js to use correct public endpoints

template_update_crash.root_cause: update_template handler had elif payload.published is not None but TemplateUpdate Pydantic model didn't define published field. When sections=None (description-only update), the elif branch was reached and accessed undefined attribute.
template_update_crash.mitigation: Added published: bool | None = None to TemplateUpdate model
